title: write/read roundtrip law is not available on native LLVM path
goal: The P7c metamorphic harness tracks the requested law `(read (write datum)) = datum`. Native Eshkol exposes line/string/char I/O but no general datum `read` through the LLVM backend, and existing evidence shows `write` does not consistently emit read syntax for strings. Counterexample: `(write "hi")` produces native output `"hi"` as raw text without read-syntax quotes, so a datum reader could not distinguish it from the symbol/token hi. This keeps write/read identity unsupported for strings and compound data containing strings.
acceptance: Native `eshkol-run -r` exposes a general datum `read` that can consume text produced by `write`.
`(write "hi")` emits read syntax with quotes/escapes, and `(read (open-input-string <that text>))` reconstructs the original string.
The P7c metamorphic write/read XKNOWN is converted to a normal PASS check.
campaign_payoff: Write/read identity is a core Scheme persistence and reproducibility law; leaving it explicit prevents roundtrip gaps from being hidden under line-oriented I/O tests.

verifications.evidence: scripts/run_metamorphic.sh --quick --no-aot reports XKNOWN roundtrip.write_read.string.case0 task=ESH-0170 and otherwise passes the quick JIT gate.
